data.content:
# Implementation Plan — foolery-3c44

## Summary
Add a single-server-process beats-sync coordinator with two HTTP endpoints
(`POST` triggers idempotently, `GET` is read-only), persist a new
`autoSync` boolean setting (default `false`), and wire a frontend poller
plus a toggle into the settings sheet. The sync algorithm shuffles
registered Knots/Beads repos and sleeps a random 2–20 s between
per-repo `kno sync` / `bd sync` invocations to avoid a thundering herd.

## Scope decisions
1. `autoSync` is a top-level boolean setting (peer of
   `terminalLightTheme`, `maxConcurrentSessions`), not nested under
   `defaults`. It is a behaviour knob, not a beat-creation default.
2. Existing startup hook `backfillMissingSettingsDefaults` (called from
   `src/instrumentation.ts`) already writes any missing setting that has
   a schema default. Adding `autoSync` to the schema is therefore
   sufficient to satisfy AC #6 — no additional bootstrap call needed.
3. Singleton sync state lives in a module-level object in the Next.js
   server process. Foolery is a single-process desktop app, so a
   module-level CAS-style guard is sufficient. Multi-instance deploys
   are out of scope.
4. Frontend toggle goes in the existing Defaults tab of the settings
   sheet, near `terminalLightTheme`. (Repos tab is also defensible, but
   Defaults already houses cross-cutting behaviour switches.)
5. Per-repo CLI invocation matrix:
   - `memoryManagerType === "knots"` → `kno sync` (cwd = repo path)
   - `memoryManagerType === "beads"` → `bd sync --no-daemon`
6. Failures from a single repo's sync MUST NOT abort the job. Each repo
   is independently logged; `lastSyncedAt` is updated only on success.
7. Per-call dependency injection is mandatory for the runner so tests
   stay hermetic (no real CLI, no real timers, no real PRNG).

## File changes

### New files

**`src/lib/beats-sync-state.ts`** (~80 lines)
Module-level singleton state and accessors.
- `interface BeatsSyncProjectState { repoPath: string; lastSyncedAt: string | null }`
- `interface BeatsSyncState { running: boolean; projects: BeatsSyncProjectState[] }`
- Functions:
  - `getBeatsSyncStateSnapshot(): BeatsSyncState` — deep-clone projects
  - `setBeatsSyncRunning(running: boolean): void`
  - `isBeatsSyncRunning(): boolean`
  - `upsertBeatsSyncProject(repoPath: string): BeatsSyncProjectState`
  - `markBeatsSyncProjectSucceeded(repoPath: string, isoTimestamp: string): void`
  - `_resetBeatsSyncStateForTests(): void`
- Internal state is a single `BeatsSyncState` object held in a closure;
  per-repo `lastSyncedAt` is preserved across runs (last successful
  timestamp wins).

**`src/lib/beats-sync-runner.ts`** (~100 lines)
Per-repo CLI invocation, isolated for injection in tests.
- `interface BeatsSyncRunnerDeps { execFile, now }`
- `runRepoSync(repo: RegisteredRepo, deps: BeatsSyncRunnerDeps): Promise<{ ok: boolean; error?: string }>`
- Uses `kno sync` for knots, `bd sync --no-daemon` for beads.
- Reports failures to `serverLog("warn", "beats-sync", ...)` and
  continues; never throws.

**`src/lib/beats-sync-service.ts`** (~180 lines)
Public sync orchestrator. Exports:
- `getBeatsSyncState(): Promise<BeatsSyncState>`
  - Hydrates `projects` from `listRepos()` (lazily upsert any new repos
    with `lastSyncedAt: null`) and returns a snapshot.
- `triggerBeatsSync(opts?): Promise<BeatsSyncState>`
  - Captures pre-state via `getBeatsSyncState()`.
  - If `isBeatsSyncRunning()`, returns the pre-state untouched.
  - Otherwise sets `running = true` atomically and `void`-launches
    `runBeatsSyncJob(deps)` (does NOT await — caller must see the
    pre-state immediately).
  - Returns the captured pre-state.
- `runBeatsSyncJob(deps)` (private)
  - Loads registered repos, filters to knots/beads only.
  - Fisher-Yates shuffle using injected `random` function.
  - Iterates sequentially; between iterations sleeps for
    `2_000 + random() * 18_000` ms using injected `delay`.
  - Calls `runRepoSync` for each repo; on success, writes
    `markBeatsSyncProjectSucceeded(repoPath, now().toISOString())`.
  - In `finally`, clears `running`.
- Injection surface:
  ```ts
  export interface BeatsSyncDeps {
    listRepos?: () => Promise<RegisteredRepo[]>;
    runRepoSync?: (repo) => Promise<...>;
    delay?: (ms: number) => Promise<void>;
    random?: () => number; // [0,1)
    now?: () => Date;
  }
  ```
- Default deps wire to `listRepos` from `registry`, real `runRepoSync`,
  `setTimeout`-backed delay, `Math.random`, `() => new Date()`.

**`src/app/api/sync/beats/route.ts`** (~50 lines)
Thin Next.js handler:
- `GET` → `NextResponse.json(await getBeatsSyncState())`
- `POST` → `NextResponse.json(await triggerBeatsSync())`
- Both wrap with `logApiError` on throw and return 500.

**`src/lib/sync-beats-api.ts`** (~30 lines)
Browser-side fetch wrappers: `fetchSyncState()`, `triggerSyncRun()`,
mirroring `BeatsSyncState`.

**`src/hooks/use-auto-sync.ts`** (~50 lines)
Client hook:
- Reads `useQuery(["settings"], fetchSettings)` to detect `autoSync`.
- When `settings.autoSync === true`, uses `useEffect` + `setInterval`
  (3 minutes = 180_000 ms) to call `triggerSyncRun`. On unmount or when
  the setting flips false, clears the interval.
- Returns `null`; mount once in `providers.tsx`.
- Fires one immediate trigger when the setting flips on (so the first
  cycle happens within a couple of seconds, not 3 minutes).

**`src/lib/openapi/paths-sync.ts`** (~110 lines)
Define `/api/sync/beats` GET and POST paths plus a `BeatsSyncState`
component schema; register in the umbrella `openapi/paths-*` aggregator
(see how `paths-system.ts` is wired in `src/app/api/openapi.json/route.ts`).

### Tests (hermetic)

**`src/lib/__tests__/beats-sync-service.test.ts`** (~200 lines)
- 15-fold concurrent `triggerBeatsSync()` → exactly one call into the
  injected `runRepoSync`. Use a deferred `delay` (resolves when test
  manually invokes a `release()` callback) so the run stays in flight
  while the 14 follow-ups race.
- Pre-trigger state has `running: false`; post-second-trigger state has
  `running: true` and does NOT relaunch.
- Shuffle uses injected PRNG and visits all repos exactly once.
- Delay is invoked N-1 times with values in `[2000, 20000)`.
- `lastSyncedAt` per-repo updates on success; failing repos retain prior
  `null` / prior value.
- GET-equivalent (`getBeatsSyncState`) does not call the runner.

**`src/lib/__tests__/sync-beats-route.test.ts`** (~80 lines)
- vi.mock `@/lib/beats-sync-service`.
- POST → response equals mocked `triggerBeatsSync` return; trigger
  invoked exactly once per request.
- GET → response equals mocked `getBeatsSyncState`; trigger NOT invoked.
- Error path: service throws → 500 with error body.

**`src/lib/__tests__/settings-autosync.test.ts`** (~80 lines)
- Schema parses with `autoSync` absent → defaults to `false`.
- Schema parses with `autoSync: true` → preserved.
- `mergeSettingsPartial` toggles `autoSync` without disturbing other
  fields.
- `backfillMissingSettingsDefaults` writes `autoSync = false` to disk
  when the on-disk TOML lacks it (use the existing `mockReadFile`/
  `mockWriteFile` pattern from `settings-load.test.ts`).

No frontend timer test in the default suite — `setInterval` is
non-hermetic. If we need coverage, add a manual test under
`__manual_tests__/` exercising the hook with `vi.useFakeTimers()`.

### Modified files

- `src/lib/schemas.ts` — add `autoSync` to `foolerySettingsSchema`:
  ```ts
  autoSync: z.boolean().default(false).describe(
    "Auto-trigger periodic beats sync from the frontend (~3 min). When "
    + "false, the frontend never polls /api/sync/beats. Default: false.",
  ),
  ```
- `src/lib/settings-types.ts` — extend `SettingsPartial` with
  `autoSync?: boolean`.
- `src/lib/settings-update.ts` — merge `partial.autoSync` analogously to
  `terminalLightTheme`.
- `src/components/settings-sheet.tsx` — thread `autoSync` through
  `SettingsData`, `DEFAULTS`, `hydrateSettings`, and into the Defaults
  panel props.
- `src/components/settings-defaults-section.tsx` — add an
  `AutoSyncSection` styled like `TerminalThemeSection` with a `Switch`.
- `src/components/providers.tsx` — mount `<AutoSyncPoller />` (a tiny
  wrapper around `useAutoSync()`) inside the query-client provider so
  it has access to React Query and only renders client-side.
- `src/app/api/openapi.json/route.ts` — register the new sync paths.

## Test strategy
- Hermetic unit tests cover all 9 acceptance criteria except #7 (UI
  persistence) and #8 (frontend polling behaviour). For #7 we verify
  the round-trip through `mergeSettingsPartial` plus the settings load
  defaults test; restarting the app is the schema-default test. For #8
  we verify the hook's logical contract via mock review at PR time —
  the runtime behaviour is too coupled to React Query / `setInterval`
  to test hermetically. Note as a known gap in the handoff.
- `bun run lint`, `bunx tsc --noEmit`, `bun run test:all`, `bun run build`
  before transitioning to plan_review.

## Complexity & risks
- **Complexity:** Medium. ~9 new files (~700 LOC), 6 modified files.
  Core logic (sync coordinator) is the only non-trivial part; rest is
  CRUD scaffolding.
- **Race risk:** Module-level `running` flag is safe under Node's single-
  threaded event loop because the read-and-set happens within a single
  synchronous turn before any `await`. The 15-concurrent test pins this.
- **CLI compatibility:** `kno sync` and `bd sync --no-daemon` are
  already used elsewhere in the codebase (see `bd-update.ts`); no new
  CLI surface to verify.
- **Settings cache invalidation:** `loadSettings` uses a 30-second TTL
  cache. A toggle save will be visible to the frontend via React
  Query's settings refetch, but the server-side `useAutoSync` is purely
  client-driven so server cache isn't on this path.
- **Frontend polling correctness:** `setInterval` is cleared on
  unmount and on `autoSync` flipping false; the hook re-runs its effect
  on settings change because the dep array includes `settings.autoSync`.

## Invariants
None declared on this knot. The plan respects the Foolery overrides:
- No hardcoded state names (sync touches no workflow descriptors).
- "Fail loudly" only governs dispatch resolution; sync is not a dispatch
  path. Per-repo CLI failures are logged via `serverLog` and surfaced
  in the response's `lastSyncedAt` (which stays `null` or stale).
